titleStmt.title: Letter from the Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, to Ulick Joseph Burke, 23 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Typescript letter informing Ulick Joseph Burke that RCPI have received details of the grounds upon which the Medical Council removed his name from the Medical Register, and that 'if evidence of further misconduct on your part be forthcoming' RCPI will considered depriving him of his Licentiate diplomas. (See letters RCPI/2/3/2/13/130, RCPI/2/3/2/13/133 - 134, RCPI/2/3/2/13/194, RCPI/2/3/1/58/T47 and RCPI/2/3/1/58/T94)

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.persName: Fellow and Registrar, Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
RCPI/2/3/2/13/139 23rd DECEMBER 15.
U.J.Burke, Esq., L.R.C.P. & S.I., Stratford, London, E. Sir, The President and Fellows of the Royal College of
Physicians of Ireland have received from the General
Medical Council details of the grounds upon which the
Council has removed your name from the Medical Register,
and I am directed by the President and Fellows to warn
you that if evidence of further misconduct on your part
be forthcoming they will have to consider the question
of depriving you of the diplomas you hold as a Licentiate
of the College. I am, Sir,
Yours
Fellow & Registrar.
